rag_rules: Insert AGREE annex blocks into the target implementation scope only when bindings depend on implementation-level details, not component type interfaces.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope boundaries: component type annexes must use only visible ports, not subcomponent paths.
Implementation annexes may bind to subcomponents but must not reference AADL properties as if they were AGREE variables.
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE annexes must be enclosed in `annex agree {** ... **};`.
Use `eq` to define boolean range predicates from visible inputs/outputs, not AADL properties.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL properties (e.g., Period, Deadline) as variables in AGREE expressions.
Do not use subcomponent port names in component type annexes.
irrelevant_topics: type scope range assumption and guarantee
AADL Property Names Are Not AGREE Variables
